seoTitle: How to Use Plans in Jira for Multi-Team Work
metaDescription: Learn how to create and use Plans in Jira to add projects and boards, build timelines, estimate capacity, and track multi-team work.
intro: Planning across multiple teams and boards is hard without a tool built for that scale. This guide covers creating and using Plans in Jira — adding projects and boards, building a timeline, and estimating capacity across teams.

steps.heading: Create a new plan
steps.body: Start a new Plan in Jira, which is designed to combine work from multiple projects and boards into one view.

steps.heading: Add projects and boards
steps.body: Bring in the specific projects and boards you want to plan across so all relevant work shows up in one place.

steps.heading: Build the timeline and dependencies
steps.body: Lay out your schedule on the timeline and map dependencies between work items across different teams.

steps.heading: Estimate capacity and track progress
steps.body: Set capacity estimates for your teams and monitor progress against the plan as work moves forward.

faq.question: What is a Plan in Jira?
faq.answer: A Plan is a feature that lets you combine multiple projects and boards into a single timeline for multi-team planning and forecasting.

faq.question: How do I add multiple projects to a Jira Plan?
faq.answer: When setting up or editing a Plan, you can add the specific projects and boards you want included in that view.

faq.question: Can Jira Plans help with capacity planning?
faq.answer: Yes, Plans let you estimate team capacity and schedules so you can forecast realistic delivery timelines across teams.

faq.question: Who typically uses Jira Plans?
faq.answer: Project managers, product owners, and Jira administrators use Plans to coordinate work across multiple teams and projects.
